sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.90 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A profinite group in which all centralizers of non-trivial elements are pronilpotent is called a CN-group. Find an example of a finitely generated infinite profinite CN-group which is not prosoluble. The structure of profinite CN-groups is described in (P.\,Shumyatsky, Israel J.~Math., 235, no.\,1 (2020), 325--347). { P.\,Shumyatsky}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
